pi_items.source: Task description specifies label should be 'California State Sales Tax', which contains 'California'
Drupal Commerce core tax module provides \Drupal\commerce_tax\Plugin\Commerce\TaxType\Custom plugin class (https://drupalcommerce.org/blog/31036/commerce-2x-stories-taxes)

Drupal Commerce documentation confirms 'Display taxes of this type inclusive in product prices' is a valid tax type configuration field (https://docs.drupalcommerce.org/v2/user-guide/taxes/). Task specifies this should be unchecked (false).
ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 standard country code for United States. Task specifies 'United States > California'.
ISO 3166-2 standard administrative area code for California. Used in Drupal Commerce zone configuration for territorial matching.

California state sales tax rate is confirmed as 7.25% (0.0725) by official California Department of Tax and Fee Administration (https://cdtfa.ca.gov/taxes-and-fees/rates.aspx). This is the base statewide sales tax rate.
Task description explicitly specifies tax rate label should be 'Standard' in the zone configuration.

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims are verified and accurate. The California state sales tax rate of 0.0725 (7.25%) is the official statewide rate. The 'custom' plugin type is a core Drupal Commerce tax type plugin. The configuration fields (display_inclusive, zones, territories, rates) are standard Drupal Commerce tax type entity properties. Country code 'US' and administrative area 'CA' are ISO standard codes. The task requires manually creating a tax type configuration in the Drupal Commerce admin UI, so the data is synthetically created as part of the task (not imported from external sources), but the underlying tax rate value is based on real California tax law.
